items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## üéØ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This issue documents a documentation gap in the `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` package README ‚Äî the install snippet references the wrong package name (`Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI`), and the Toolboxes section is missing several operations (client setup, Update, List versions, List all, Delete).

<details>
<summary>üìã Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` (prerelease)
**Affected API:** `AgentToolboxes` client ‚Äî `GetAgentToolboxes()`, `UpdateToolbox`, `GetToolboxVersions`, `GetToolboxes`, `DeleteToolboxVersion`, `DeleteToolbox`
**Scenarios:**
- Wrong `dotnet add package` command in the "Install the package" section (references `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` instead of `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`)
- README Toolboxes section missing client instantiation via `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()`
- README Toolboxes section missing Update, List (versions + all toolboxes), and Delete operations despite test file snippets already existing

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` to fix the install snippet and add the missing Toolboxes sub-sections using existing snippet regions from `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>üîé Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no runtime or API surface change is required. The implementation guide in the issue body is detailed and actionable ‚Äî snippet regions already exist in the test file.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Fix the `dotnet add package` command in the README "Install the package" section (`Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` ‚Üí `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`).
2. Insert the missing Toolboxes sub-sections into the README using snippet placeholders matching the `#region` names in `Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`.
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` and verify the snippet placeholders are populated correctly via `git diff`.
4. Run `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` and `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm nothing is broken.
</details>

<details>
<summary>üè∑Ô∏è Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` ‚Äî the package `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` is a data-plane client SDK (not management or provisioning)
- **Service:** `AI Projects` ‚Äî the package namespace (`Azure.AI.Projects.*`) and service directory (`sdk/ai/`) map to the `AI Projects` service label, consistent with similar issues (#56436, #52355)
- **Confidence:** High ‚Äî the package name is explicit in the issue body, the category and service are unambiguous, and the label selection aligns with quality reference issues
</details>
